2604.07763.title: Beyond Surface Artifacts: Capturing Shared Latent Forgery Knowledge Across Modalities
2604.07763.paper_summary: As generative artificial intelligence evolves, deepfake attacks have escalated from single-modality manipulations to complex, multimodal threats. Existing forensic techniques face a severe generalization bottleneck: by relying excessively on superficial, modality-specific artifacts, they neglect the shared latent forgery knowledge hidden beneath variable physical appearances. Consequently, these models suffer catastrophic performance degradation when confronted with unseen "dark modalities." To break this limitation, this paper introduces a paradigm shift that redefines multimodal forensics from conventional "feature fusion" to "modality generalization." We propose the first modality-agnostic forgery (MAF) detection framework. By explicitly decoupling modality-specific styles, MAF precisely extracts the essential, cross-modal latent forgery knowledge. Furthermore, we define two progressive dimensions to quantify model generalization: transferability toward semantically correlated modalities (Weak MAF), and robustness against completely isolated signals of "dark modality" (Strong MAF). To rigorously assess these generalization limits, we introduce the DeepModal-Bench benchmark, which integrates diverse multimodal forgery detection algorithms and adapts state-of-the-art generalized learning methods. This study not only empirically proves the existence of universal forgery traces but also achieves significant performance breakthroughs on unknown modalities via the MAF framework, offering a pioneering technical pathway for universal multimodal defense.
2604.07763.paper_summary_zh: 隨著生成性人工智慧的演變，深偽攻擊已經從單一模態的操控升級為複雜的多模態威脅。現有的取證技術面臨嚴重的泛化瓶頸：由於過度依賴表面的、特定模態的工件，它們忽略了隱藏在變化的物理外觀下的共享潛在偽造知識。因此，這些模型在面對未見的「黑暗模態」時會遭受災難性的性能降級。為了突破這一限制，本文引入了一個範式轉變，將多模態取證從傳統的「特徵融合」重新定義為「模態泛化」。我們提出了首個模態無關的偽造（MAF）檢測框架。通過明確解耦模態特定的風格，MAF 精確提取了基本的跨模態潛在偽造知識。此外，我們定義了兩個進步的維度來量化模型的泛化能力：對語義相關模態的可轉移性（弱 MAF），以及對完全孤立的「黑暗模態」信號的魯棒性（強 MAF）。為了嚴格評估這些泛化極限，我們引入了 DeepModal-Bench 基準，該基準整合了多樣的多模態偽造檢測算法並適應最先進的泛化學習方法。本研究不僅實證證明了普遍偽造痕跡的存在，還通過 MAF 框架在未知模態上取得了顯著的性能突破，為普遍多模態防禦提供了一條開創性的技術途徑。
2604.07763.author: Jingtong Dou et.al.
2604.07763.authors: Jingtong Dou, Chuancheng Shi, Jian Wang, Fei Shen, Zhiyong Wang, Tat-Seng Chua
